message:
[high] google-calendar: pin base_url to googleapis.com hosts

from_params accepted any base_url override with zero validation,
so a configure payload could redirect Calendar API traffic to any
host. No scheme check, no host allowlist — the previous code was
strictly weaker than the gmail parse_base_url path.

- New validate_calendar_base_url() rejects non-googleapis.com
  hosts (subdomain-suffix match, so googleapis.com.evil.com and
  evil-googleapis.com are both rejected) unless the override
  targets a local test host. Parses the URL so substring-smuggle
  payloads like https://evil.com/calendar.googleapis.com fail.
- Plain http is rejected except on localhost/127.0.0.1/::1.
- from_params now trims and rejects empty base_url overrides so
  " " cannot silently fall through to the client default.
- Seven new unit tests cover accept (apex + subdomain), local
  http, foreign host, substring smuggle, http on public host,
  empty/malformed, and a host_is_googleapis truth-table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
